title: Landolphia myrtifolia
uses: Landolphia myrtifolia is used in traditional medicine, primarily in West and Central Africa. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can aid in treating various infections. In some cultures, the bark of the plant is used to treat fever and rheumatism. Additionally, the plant's leaves are used to treat wounds and skin conditions. Further research is necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Landolphia myrtifolia for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: I can answer that question. Landolphia myrtifolia is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. It is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree native to tropical Africa. The genus Landolphia is part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae, and it is closely related to other genera such as Rauvolfia and Carissa. Landolphia myrtifolia is a highly variable species with different subspecies and varieties found in various parts of its range.

side_effects: Landolphia myrtifolia is known to be toxic, and its side effects can be severe. Ingestion of the plant can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. intro: Landolphia myrtifolia, also known as the bitter cherry or bitter yolk, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Landolphia myrtifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant is often used to make traditional herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to manage various health conditions in African folk medicine.
